---
title: "ev — ExaVerif"
subtitle: "Exhaustive Verification for RISC-V Custom Instructions"
date: last-modified
metadata-files:
  - _include/format.html.yml
abstract: |
  ev (ExaVerif) is an open-source (Apache 2.0) exhaustive verification CLI for RISC-V custom instruction extensions. Given a YAML file describing instruction fields and their constraints, ev generates every valid combination, checks them deterministically, and reports which pass and which fail.
---

## Quick Start

```bash
# Full CI pipeline
./run.sh

# Channel demo: cross-verify against SSCCS POC golden anchors
./run.sh --demo

# Verify an instruction
ev check --target tests/fixtures/all_pass.xif.yaml

# Verify with cross-field constraint and JSON output
ev check --target tests/fixtures/sample.xif.yaml --json
```

## Architecture

```text
src/
  main.rs         CLI (clap: check, certify)
  spec.rs         VerificationSpec — format-agnostic internal IR
  format.rs       FormatCapable trait
  xif.rs          YamlFormat — RISCV-CTG-compatible YAML parser
  compose.rs      Domain expansion (cartesian product)
  evaluate.rs     Field construction + observe() pipeline
  registry.rs     ConstraintRegistry + ProjectorRegistry (pluggable)
  reporter.rs     ReporterCapable trait + TextReporter + JsonReporter
```

Capability-trait architecture (same pattern as [NeXus](https://docs.ssccs.org/projects/nexus/)):

| Extension Point | How |
|:---|:---|
| New constraint | `ConstraintRegistry::register("name", builder)` |
| New projector | `ProjectorRegistry::register("name", builder)` |
| New input format | Implement `FormatCapable` |
| New output format | Implement `ReporterCapable` |

## Input Format

ev uses YAML -- the industry standard in RISC-V verification (RISCV-CTG, RISCV-DV, RISCV-Config all use YAML). `.ss` (SSCCS native) is planned as a future format.

```yaml
target: simple_alu
fields:
  op_a:    { range: [0, 15] }
  op_b:    { range: [0, 15] }
  op_code: { values: [0, 1, 2, 3] }
constraints:
  - type: eq
    axis_a: 0
    axis_b: 1
projector: { type: sum }
```

## Channel Demo

ev independently cross-verifies against hand-written RISC-V assembly from the [SSCCS PoC](https://github.com/ssccsorg/ssccs):

```text
narrow:   even ∧ range_0_10  →  2,REJECT,REJECT,10,REJECT  ✓
broad:    no constraints     →  2,3,5,10,12                ✓
sum3d_a:  (2,1,0)            →  3                          ✓
sum3d_b:  (1,2,3)            →  6                          ✓
parity:   {2,3}              →  0,1                        ✓
```

Same constraints, same results -- two completely independent paths: handwritten RISC-V assembly vs. Rust-based exhaustive verification.

## Triple-Substrate Validation

The SSCCS PoC that ev cross-verifies against implements the same constraint pipeline across three independent substrates:

```text
RISC-V Assembly (.S)  ──┐
                         ├── Golden Anchors (19, auto-verified by Python)
SystemVerilog (.sv)    ──┤
                         │
Rust Fallback          ──┘
```

A Python script (`check_golden_anchors.py`) automatically verifies all 19 golden constants. Any change to the assembly forces a cross-substrate consistency check.

## Ecosystem

ev is part of the [SSCCS Foundation](https://ssccs.org) ecosystem:

- **SSCCS PoC** ([github.com/ssccsorg/ssccs](https://github.com/ssccsorg/ssccs)) -- Field Composition engine, Scheme abstraction layer, 18 crates
- **NeXus** ([docs.ssccs.org/projects/nexus](https://docs.ssccs.org/projects/nexus/)) -- OODA loop orchestration engine
- **ev (ExaVerif)** ([github.com/ssccsorg/ev](https://github.com/ssccsorg/ev)) -- exhaustive verification CLI (this project)

## License

Apache 2.0. Copyright 2026 [SSCCS Foundation](https://ssccs.org).
